report_name: Puerto Rico Agency Denies Data Leak After Exposing 1 Million Social Security Numbers

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: A broken access control vulnerability (IDOR) in the backend API of the 'Catastro Digital' online map service allowed unauthenticated users to access sensitive data.

attack_patterns.name: Automated Collection
attack_patterns.description: The IDOR vulnerability enabled the potential for automated collection of approximately 1 million Social Security numbers by sequentially scanning numeric IDs in the insecure API endpoint.

vulnerabilities.name: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) in Catastro Digital API
vulnerabilities.description: A broken access control vulnerability, specifically an Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR), in the backend API for the 'Catastro Digital' map service allowed any user to directly query and download sensitive personal information, including Social Security numbers, without authentication.
affected_products: CRIM Catastro Digital online property map service

identities.name: Municipal Revenue Collection Center (CRIM)
identities.description: The Puerto Rican government agency responsible for municipal revenue collection whose 'Catastro Digital' service inadvertently exposed the personal data of approximately 1 million people.

identities.name: Citizens of Puerto Rico
identities.description: A class representing the approximately 1 million people whose Social Security numbers were exposed due to the vulnerability in CRIM's 'Catastro Digital' service.

indicators.name: Potential IDOR Enumeration on API Endpoint
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential IDOR Enumeration on API Endpoint
id: 2b8a1c3d-f4e5-4b9a-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects access to API endpoints with numeric IDs, which could be indicative of an Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) enumeration attempt, as described in the CRIM data exposure.
  Tuning and aggregation are likely required to reduce false positives.
references:
  - https://www.propublica.org/article/puerto-rico-crim-data-breach
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-09
tags:
  - attack.t1119
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|re: '/api/v1/(property_data|records)/\d+'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects patterns indicative of Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) enumeration, where an actor attempts to access multiple records by iterating through numeric IDs in an API endpoint like the one found in CRIM's 'Catastro Digital' service.

relationships.source_name: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) in Catastro Digital API
Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) in Catastro Digital API
Potential IDOR Enumeration on API Endpoint
